threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Underground' instead of just 'the Tube' like a true local. Also, good luck navigating the bus routes without a map!
Caught a couple arguing over whose turn it was to pay for chips at Brixton's stop—she said he owes her ever since he hogged the window seat.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw the old Kreuzberg graffiti on an Neukölln bakery's wall, with hipsters and families sharing brunch on the terrace. That’s when I knew.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find spontaneous street concerts in the middle of the night, often led by someone just passing through with a guitar and a small crowd forming.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows the quickest side streets to avoid the Muni delays when a dog gets on the tracks at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy calmly waiting for the 47, reading a book while a pigeon pecked at his shoelaces. Only in the Tenderloin do the birds have that kind of confidence.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think it’s all fancy tapas and art; they miss the small bars where locals sip vermut and gossip about the latest neighborhood drama.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll know a recent arrival by their bewilderment reading the tram schedules—everyone else just glances at Google Maps.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'Hackney' is a place instead of a badge of honour. Play it cool, mate, and don't mention the hipsters.
Last night on the 24, a guy serenaded the whole bus with 90s R&B. Even the driver cracked a smile before rolling his eyes. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy in a tutu feeding pigeons at Hermannplatz while arguing about the best döner in town. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find street art that changes weekly. It's like a live gallery—it feels alive, constantly surprising.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their nonchalant reaction to BART mishaps. The Civic Center station closing? Just another Tuesday interruption in the daily grind.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the 38 bus with a ukulele today. He sang the Farewell to the Fare Revenue song; it was oddly fitting.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists usually think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but they miss the quiet corners and local artist studios tucked away behind the busy squares.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitance to dive into the train stations during rush hour; locals know to just shoulder your way in.
